finding: Path: initially lymphomatous cells grow in interstitium; nephrons, collecting ducts, blood

differentials.raw: Acute pyelonephritis, septic emboli, renal infarct, abscess

context: vessels serve as framework for tumor expansion; with continued growth → parenchymal compression + destruction Types: Type:   typically intermediate + high-grade B-cell type At risk:   immunocompromised patients with uncontrolled Epstein-Barr virus proliferation in patients with HIV infection / organ transplantation (esp. after cyclosporine therapy), ataxia-telangiectasia (b)  PRIMARY renal lymphoma (< 1%) arising in renal hilar nodes / renal parenchyma Prevalence:   renal involvement in 13% of autopsies Patterns of involvement: ›  multiple foci (50-65%): √  multiple hypoenhancing 1-3 cm usuall
